race.name: Franches Nordique
race.tagline: Freestyle skiing through the rolling Jura pastures of Franches-Montagnes — a Swiss Loppet race in the heart of the Jura plateau.

climate.description: The Jura plateau at ~1,000m receives moderate snowfall. Temperatures typically -5°C to 2°C. Wind exposure on open pastures.
challenges: Snow reliability moderate at Jura altitude

course.primary: Rolling course through the Franches-Montagnes Jura plateau pastures and forest. Gentle terrain typical of Jura cross-country skiing.
features: Part of the Swiss Loppet series

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A small Swiss Loppet race with pleasant Jura scenery but limited reach and snow reliability.

history.summary: Franches Nordique is a Swiss Loppet series race through the Franches-Montagnes region of the Jura. The area is famous for horse breeding and its rolling plateau landscape.
notable_facts: Set in the Franches-Montagnes, known for horse breeding
Part of the Swiss Loppet series
Jura region has a long Nordic skiing tradition
